benchmarks.name: AGIEval
benchmarks.description: Human-centric benchmark from standardized exams like Gaokao, SAT, LSAT, and math competitions.

benchmarks.name: ARB
benchmarks.description: Advanced problems across mathematics, physics, law, and medicine, including symbolic and proof items graded by rubric.

benchmarks.name: ARC (AI2 Reasoning Challenge)
benchmarks.description: 7,787 grade-school science exam multiple-choice questions split into Easy and Challenge sets.

benchmarks.name: C-Eval
benchmarks.description: 13,948 Chinese multiple-choice questions across 52 disciplines and four difficulty levels.

benchmarks.name: EMMA
benchmarks.description: 2,788 problems demanding cross-modal reasoning across mathematics, physics, chemistry, and coding for multimodal models.

benchmarks.name: GAOKAO-Bench
benchmarks.description: 2,811 Chinese college-entrance-exam questions across subjects for LLM evaluation.

benchmarks.name: GPQA
benchmarks.description: 448 expert-written graduate-level biology, physics, and chemistry Google-proof multiple-choice questions.

benchmarks.name: Humanity's Last Exam
benchmarks.description: 2,500 expert questions across 100+ subjects at the frontier of human academic knowledge.

benchmarks.name: JEEBench
benchmarks.description: 515 challenging IIT-JEE Advanced physics, chemistry, and math problem-solving questions.

benchmarks.name: MMLU-Pro
benchmarks.description: 12K reasoning-focused ten-option questions across 14 academic and STEM domains.

benchmarks.name: MMMU
benchmarks.description: 11.5K college-level multimodal questions across six disciplines and 30 subjects.

benchmarks.name: OlympiadBench
benchmarks.description: 8,476 olympiad-level bilingual multimodal math and physics problems with expert step annotations.

benchmarks.name: OlympicArena
benchmarks.description: 11,163 olympiad-level problems across seven disciplines for multi-discipline cognitive reasoning.

benchmarks.name: OpenBookQA
benchmarks.description: Elementary science multiple-choice questions requiring core facts plus broad common-sense knowledge.

benchmarks.name: QASC
benchmarks.description: 9,980 grade-school science questions requiring retrieval and composition of two facts.

benchmarks.name: SciAssess
benchmarks.description: Scientific literature analysis over biology, chemistry, materials, and medicine at three levels.

benchmarks.name: SciBench
benchmarks.description: 789 open-ended college-level chemistry, physics, and math problems requiring multi-step reasoning.

benchmarks.name: ScienceQA
benchmarks.description: ~21K multimodal multiple-choice science questions with lecture and chain-of-thought explanations.

benchmarks.name: SciEval
benchmarks.description: ~18K multi-level questions testing scientific knowledge across chemistry, physics, and biology.

benchmarks.name: SciFact
benchmarks.description: 1,409 expert-written scientific claims verified against research abstracts, with rationales.

benchmarks.name: SciKnowEval
benchmarks.description: Tens of thousands of problems across five cognitive levels in biology, chemistry, physics, and materials.

benchmarks.name: SciQ
benchmarks.description: 13,679 crowdsourced science multiple-choice questions across physics, chemistry, and biology with evidence.

benchmarks.name: SuperGPQA
benchmarks.description: 26,529 graduate-level questions spanning 285 disciplines, including under-evaluated long-tail fields.

benchmarks.name: TheoremQA
benchmarks.description: 800 questions applying 350+ theorems across math, physics, EE/CS, and finance.

benchmarks.name: Xiezhi
benchmarks.description: 249,587 questions spanning 516 disciplines across 13 categories, continuously updated.

benchmarks.name: CHAMP
benchmarks.description: 270 high-school competition problems annotated with concepts and problem-specific hints.

benchmarks.name: CombiBench
benchmarks.description: 100 competition combinatorics problems formalized in Lean4, a domain underrepresented by existing formal benchmarks.

benchmarks.name: FIMO
benchmarks.description: 149 IMO-shortlist problems formalized in Lean with informal statements for olympiad-level theorem proving.

benchmarks.name: FormalMATH
benchmarks.description: 5,560 formally verified Lean4 statements from olympiad to undergraduate across algebra, calculus, and number theory.

benchmarks.name: FrontierMath
benchmarks.description: Hundreds of unpublished expert-crafted research-level math problems resistant to guessing.

benchmarks.name: GSM8K
benchmarks.description: 8.5K grade-school arithmetic word problems requiring multi-step reasoning.

benchmarks.name: HARDMath
benchmarks.description: Graduate applied-math problems requiring asymptotic and approximation methods where leading LLMs score below 45%.

benchmarks.name: HARP
benchmarks.description: US competition math problems (AMC/AIME/USAMO) with human-written ground-truth solutions.

benchmarks.name: IMO-Bench
benchmarks.description: Olympiad-level suite testing final answers, proof-writing, grading, and Lean formal proofs, vetted by IMO medalists.

benchmarks.name: Lean Workbook
benchmarks.description: About 57K formal-informal Lean4 problem pairs auto-formalized from competition math.

benchmarks.name: LeanDojo
benchmarks.description: 98,734 theorems and proofs from Lean mathlib with premise annotations for retrieval-augmented theorem proving.

benchmarks.name: MATH
benchmarks.description: 12,500 competition mathematics problems (AMC/AIME level) with step-by-step solutions.

benchmarks.name: MATH-500
benchmarks.description: 500-problem held-out subset of MATH widely used for LLM evaluation.

benchmarks.name: MathBench
benchmarks.description: Hierarchical bilingual benchmark spanning arithmetic to college-level theory and application.

benchmarks.name: MATHCHECK
benchmarks.description: Checklist benchmark testing task generalization and reasoning robustness beyond end-to-end answer accuracy.

benchmarks.name: MathOdyssey
benchmarks.description: 387 expert-crafted high-school, university, and olympiad-level math problems.

benchmarks.name: MathQA
benchmarks.description: 37K multiple-choice math word problems annotated with executable operation programs.

benchmarks.name: MathVerse
benchmarks.description: 2,612 visual math problems in six diagram/text variants probing whether MLLMs truly interpret figures.

benchmarks.name: MathVista
benchmarks.description: Mathematical reasoning benchmark combining visual contexts (figures, charts, diagrams) with problems.

benchmarks.name: miniF2F
benchmarks.description: 488 olympiad-level (AMC/AIME/IMO) problems formalized across multiple proof assistants.

benchmarks.name: Omni-MATH
benchmarks.description: 4,428 olympiad-level competition problems across 33+ subdomains and difficulty tiers.

benchmarks.name: ProofNet
benchmarks.description: 371 undergraduate theorems for autoformalization and formal proving in Lean.

benchmarks.name: Putnam-AXIOM
benchmarks.description: Putnam competition problems plus programmatically perturbed variations giving contamination-resistant unseen instances.

benchmarks.name: PutnamBench
benchmarks.description: 1,600+ Putnam competition problems formalized in Lean, Isabelle, and Coq.

benchmarks.name: We-Math
benchmarks.description: 6.5K visual math problems decomposed into 67 hierarchical knowledge concepts to diagnose reasoning versus memorization.

benchmarks.name: ABench-Physics
benchmarks.description: 500 hard static plus dynamic-variant physics problems testing reasoning and generalization robustness.

benchmarks.name: Astro-QA
benchmarks.description: About 2,700 bilingual astronomy questions across six types spanning astrophysics, celestial mechanics, and astrometry.

benchmarks.name: AstroMLab 1
benchmarks.description: 4,425 astronomy multiple-choice questions from Annual Reviews testing LLM astronomical knowledge.

benchmarks.name: AstroVisBench
benchmarks.description: Evaluates LLMs on end-to-end astronomy computing workflows and scientific result visualization.

benchmarks.name: AtmosSci-Bench
benchmarks.description: Atmospheric-science problems spanning dynamics, physics, hydrology, geophysics, and oceanography via templated questions.

benchmarks.name: CMPhysBench
benchmarks.description: 520+ graduate condensed-matter physics problems with a partial-credit metric; top models score below 30%.

benchmarks.name: Gravity-Bench-v1
benchmarks.description: Agents plan budgeted observations of simulated binary-star systems to discover the underlying gravitational physics.

benchmarks.name: HiPhO
benchmarks.description: 13 recent physics olympiad exams with step-level grading and human medal-based comparison for (M)LLMs.

benchmarks.name: LLM-SRBench
benchmarks.description: 239 problems across four science domains testing genuine LLM equation discovery over memorized formulas.

benchmarks.name: NewtonBench
benchmarks.description: 324 tasks across twelve physics domains testing LLM agents discovering scientific laws via interactive experimentation.

benchmarks.name: PHYBench
benchmarks.description: 500 original physics problems from high-school to olympiad level with expression-edit-distance scoring.

benchmarks.name: PhysGym
benchmarks.description: 97 simulated interactive physics-discovery problems with four controlled levels of prior knowledge.

benchmarks.name: PHYSICS
benchmarks.description: 1,297 expert-annotated university-level physics problems across six core areas with automated evaluation.

benchmarks.name: PhysReason
benchmarks.description: 1,200 physics problems with step-level automatic scoring for multi-step physics reasoning.

benchmarks.name: TPBench
benchmarks.description: 57 theoretical physics problems in high-energy theory and cosmology, undergraduate to research level.

benchmarks.name: UGPhysics
benchmarks.description: 5,520 bilingual undergraduate physics problems across 13 subjects with rule-based judging.

benchmarks.name: ChemBench
benchmarks.description: 2,700+ curated questions probing LLM chemical knowledge and reasoning against expert chemists.

benchmarks.name: ChemCoTBench
benchmarks.description: Step-wise chemical reasoning across molecular understanding, editing, optimization, and reaction prediction.

benchmarks.name: ChemEval
benchmarks.description: Multi-level chemistry benchmark spanning 42 tasks across four progressive difficulty levels for LLMs.

benchmarks.name: ChemLLMBench
benchmarks.description: Eight chemistry tasks including reaction prediction, retrosynthesis, and molecule captioning for LLMs.
benchmarks.org: University of Notre Dame et al.

benchmarks.name: ChemSafetyBench
benchmarks.description: Safety benchmark testing LLM handling of hazardous-chemical property, legality, and synthesis queries.

benchmarks.name: MaCBench
benchmarks.description: 1,100+ image-question pairs probing multimodal LLM limitations across chemistry and materials workflows.

benchmarks.name: MolCap-Arena
benchmarks.description: Battle-based benchmark scoring 20+ LLMs on molecule captions augmenting molecular property prediction.

benchmarks.name: MoleculeQA
benchmarks.description: 62K QA pairs over 23K molecules evaluating factual accuracy of LLM molecular comprehension.

benchmarks.name: MolTextQA
benchmarks.description: 500K QA pairs over 240K PubChem molecules evaluating molecule-structure-to-text understanding and retrieval.

benchmarks.name: MOOSE-Chem
benchmarks.description: Tests whether LLMs rediscover unseen chemistry hypotheses from 51 Nature/Science-level papers given background information.

benchmarks.name: ScholarChemQA
benchmarks.description: 40K abstract-derived chemistry research questions exposing LLM limits in comprehending scholarly literature.

benchmarks.name: SMolInstruct / LlaSMol
benchmarks.description: Large-scale instruction dataset of 14 small-molecule chemistry tasks used to train and evaluate LlaSMol.

benchmarks.name: TOMG-Bench
benchmarks.description: Evaluates LLMs on text-based open-domain molecule generation across editing, optimization, and customized generation.

benchmarks.name: ALDbench
benchmarks.description: Expert open-ended QA benchmark evaluating LLMs on atomic-layer-deposition synthesis for accuracy and specificity.

benchmarks.name: LLM4Mat-Bench
benchmarks.description: Largest benchmark for LLM materials property prediction over 1.9M crystals and 45 properties.

benchmarks.name: MaScQA
benchmarks.description: 650 undergraduate-level materials science questions across 14 categories for evaluating LLM knowledge.

benchmarks.name: MatCha
benchmarks.description: 1,500 expert questions across 21 tasks for materials characterization image understanding by multimodal models.

benchmarks.name: MatSci-NLP
benchmarks.description: Seven materials-science NLP tasks evaluating language models via a unified text-to-schema approach.

benchmarks.name: MatSciBench
benchmarks.description: College-level materials-science reasoning benchmark of 1,340 problems spanning six fields, including multimodal questions.

benchmarks.name: MatText
benchmarks.description: Benchmarking framework evaluating language models on crystal property prediction across nine text representations.

benchmarks.name: MatViX
benchmarks.description: Benchmarks vision-language models on multimodal information extraction from visually-rich materials-science articles.

benchmarks.name: MatVQA
benchmarks.description: 1,325 questions testing multimodal models on materials imagery like microscopy and diffraction with multi-step reasoning.

benchmarks.name: BioCoder
benchmarks.description: Fuzz-tested benchmark evaluating LLMs on generating bioinformatics code with cross-file dependencies and domain knowledge.

benchmarks.name: BioLLMBench
benchmarks.description: 2,160 runs evaluating GPT-4, Bard, and LLaMA across 24 bioinformatics tasks and metrics.

benchmarks.name: BioMaze
benchmarks.description: 5.1K real-research pathway problems testing LLM reasoning over biological pathways and perturbations.

benchmarks.name: BioML-bench
benchmarks.description: AI agents build end-to-end biomedical ML pipelines across protein, omics, imaging, and drug tasks.

benchmarks.name: Biomni
benchmarks.description: General biomedical agent with Biomni-Eval: 433 instances across 10 reasoning tasks.

benchmarks.name: BioMysteryBench
benchmarks.description: 99 expert-written bioinformatics tasks over raw datasets, judged on the final biological conclusion, not the path.

benchmarks.name: BioPlanner
benchmarks.description: BioProt dataset automatically evaluating LLMs on generating biology experimental protocols as executable pseudocode.

benchmarks.name: BioProBench
benchmarks.description: Tests LLMs on biological-protocol QA, step ordering, error correction, generation, and reasoning across 556K instances.

benchmarks.name: BixBench
benchmarks.description: Bioinformatics agents tackle 53 real analysis scenarios with ~300 open-answer research questions.

benchmarks.name: CellVoyager
benchmarks.description: CellBench: 76 scRNA-seq studies test agents predicting which analyses the authors performed.

benchmarks.name: GeneBench-Pro
benchmarks.description: 129 messy, judgment-intensive computational-biology problems across ten genomics domains on realistic datasets.

benchmarks.name: GeneGPT
benchmarks.description: Teaches LLMs to call NCBI Web APIs; adds GeneHop, evaluated on GeneTuring.

benchmarks.name: GeneTuring
benchmarks.description: 16 genomics tasks, 1,600 questions probing LLM genomic knowledge and reasoning.

benchmarks.name: Genome-Bench
benchmarks.description: 3,000+ genome-engineering multiple-choice questions mined from expert forum discussions for evaluating LLM reasoning.

benchmarks.name: GenoTEX
benchmarks.description: Expert-curated gene-expression tasks: dataset selection, preprocessing, and gene-trait statistical analysis.

benchmarks.name: HealthBench
benchmarks.description: 5,000 multi-turn physician-crafted health conversations scored by rubric criteria for LLM performance and safety.

benchmarks.name: LAB-Bench
benchmarks.description: 2,400+ MCQs across literature, figures, databases, protocols, and DNA/protein sequence tasks.

benchmarks.name: LifeSciBench
benchmarks.description: 750 expert-authored free-response life-science research tasks across seven biological domains, rubric-graded.

benchmarks.name: ProteinLMBench
benchmarks.description: 944 verified MCQs assessing LLM comprehension of protein sequences and descriptions.

benchmarks.name: AAAR-1.0
benchmarks.description: Expert-annotated tasks assessing LLMs on equation inference, experiment design, and paper-weakness identification.

benchmarks.name: AstaBench
benchmarks.description: 2,400+ problems across eleven benchmarks evaluating agents over the full scientific research pipeline.

benchmarks.name: BLADE
benchmarks.description: Twelve datasets and research questions evaluating agents' analytical decisions against expert ground truth.

benchmarks.name: CORE-Bench
benchmarks.description: 270 tasks from 90 papers testing agents on computationally reproducing published scientific results.

benchmarks.name: Curie
benchmarks.description: Framework and 46-question benchmark for rigorous, automated scientific experimentation across four CS domains.

benchmarks.name: DiscoveryBench
benchmarks.description: 264 real plus 903 synthetic tasks for data-driven hypothesis discovery across six domains.

benchmarks.name: DiscoveryWorld
benchmarks.description: Simulated environment with 120 tasks requiring full cycles of hypothesis, experiment, and analysis.

benchmarks.name: DSBench
benchmarks.description: 540 realistic data-analysis and data-modeling tasks sourced from ModelOff and Kaggle competitions.

benchmarks.name: EXP-Bench
benchmarks.description: Benchmarks agents on designing, implementing, and analyzing end-to-end AI research experiments from publications.

benchmarks.name: InnovatorBench
benchmarks.description: Evaluates agents on end-to-end innovative LLM-research tasks like data construction, loss design, and reward design.

benchmarks.name: LiveIdeaBench
benchmarks.description: Benchmarks scientific idea generation from minimal keyword context across originality, feasibility, and clarity.

benchmarks.name: LMR-Bench
benchmarks.description: 28 tasks from 23 NLP papers testing whether agents reproduce masked research code, verified by unit tests.

benchmarks.name: MLAgentBench
benchmarks.description: 13 ML experimentation tasks where agents read, write, and execute code to improve performance.

benchmarks.name: MLE-bench
benchmarks.description: 75 Kaggle ML-engineering competitions testing agents against human leaderboards.

benchmarks.name: MLGym
benchmarks.description: Gym framework with 13 open-ended AI research tasks spanning vision, NLP, RL, and game theory.

benchmarks.name: MLR-Bench
benchmarks.description: 201 workshop-derived tasks evaluating agents across idea generation, experimentation, and paper writing.

benchmarks.name: MLRC-Bench
benchmarks.description: Benchmarks agents on proposing and coding novel methods for seven recent ML research competition problems.

benchmarks.name: Paper2Code
benchmarks.description: Multi-agent framework and benchmark generating functional code repositories that reproduce ML research papers.

benchmarks.name: PaperBench
benchmarks.description: Agents replicate 20 ICML 2024 papers from scratch, graded on 8,316 rubric criteria.

benchmarks.name: RE-Bench
benchmarks.description: Seven open-ended ML research-engineering environments comparing agents against 61 human experts.

benchmarks.name: ResearchBench
benchmarks.description: Benchmarks scientific discovery via inspiration retrieval, hypothesis composition, and ranking across twelve disciplines.

benchmarks.name: ResearchCodeBench
benchmarks.description: Challenges LLMs to implement novel contributions from recent ML papers by completing TODO code snippets.

benchmarks.name: RExBench
benchmarks.description: Twelve tasks where agents autonomously implement novel research extensions to existing papers' codebases.

benchmarks.name: SciCode
benchmarks.description: 80 real research coding problems across 16 natural-science subfields, split into 338 subproblems.

benchmarks.name: ScienceAgentBench
benchmarks.description: 102 data-driven discovery tasks from 44 peer-reviewed papers, evaluating agents that write Python programs.

benchmarks.name: Scientist-Bench
benchmarks.description: Evaluates fully autonomous idea-to-paper research systems across CV, NLP, data mining, and IR against expert papers.

benchmarks.name: SciReplicate-Bench
benchmarks.description: Benchmarks agents on reproducing executable code for algorithms described in 36 recent research papers.

benchmarks.name: SUPER
benchmarks.description: Evaluates agents on setting up and executing tasks from research code repositories.
